top_features.human_summary: Longer URLs generally increase phishing likelihood.
Reply-to mismatch is a strong phishing indicator.
Domains impersonating known brands highly correlate with phishing.
Urgent language is frequently used in phishing attacks.
High use of exclamation marks slightly increases risk.

false_positives: Legitimate business emails with urgent wording and multiple links are sometimes misclassified as phishing.
Automated system alerts from unrecognized internal domains may trigger high risk scores.
false_negatives: Phishing emails with clean-looking, compromised sender domains and short URLs are occasionally missed.
Spear-phishing lacking urgent language and containing no links (e.g. BEC requesting a reply) can evade detection.
